title: Power Redundancy Compliance (N+1)
spl:
index=power sourcetype="power:redundancy"
| where feed_a_ok=0 OR feed_b_ok=0 OR load_on_loss_of_one_pct > 100
| table _time, pdu_id, feed_a_ok, feed_b_ok, load_on_loss_of_one_pct
description: Proves both A/B feeds and redundant UPS paths are energized and within load limits for loss-of-one scenarios.
value: Proves both A/B feeds and redundant UPS paths are energized and within load limits for loss-of-one scenarios.
implementation: Model expected load after single failure from DCIM. Alert when any feed is down or modeled headroom <0%. Pair with physical walk-through audits.
visualization: Status grid (feed × PDU), Gauge (headroom %), Table (non-compliant PDUs).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: PDU/ATS telemetry, DCIM rules.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="power:redundancy"` (feed_a_ok, feed_b_ok, load_on_loss_of_one_pct).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Model expected load after single failure from DCIM. Alert when any feed is down or modeled headroom <0%. Pair with physical walk-through audits.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="power:redundancy"
| where feed_a_ok=0 OR feed_b_ok=0 OR load_on_loss_of_one_pct > 100
| table _time, pdu_id, feed_a_ok, feed_b_ok, load_on_loss_of_one_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Power Redundancy Compliance (N+1)** — Proves both A/B feeds and redundant UPS paths are energized and within load limits for loss-of-one scenarios.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="power:redundancy"` (feed_a_ok, feed_b_ok, load_on_loss_of_one_pct). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): PDU/ATS telemetry, DCIM rules. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: power:redundancy. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="power:redundancy". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where feed_a_ok=0 OR feed_b_ok=0 OR load_on_loss_of_one_pct > 100` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Power Redundancy Compliance (N+1)**): table _time, pdu_id, feed_a_ok, feed_b_ok, load_on_loss_of_one_pct


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (feed × PDU), Gauge (headroom %), Table (non-compliant PDUs).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Power spikes during equipment power cycling, scheduled load shifting, or DR drills.
